Liberty Hyde Bailey papers, 1854-20041870-1958
Collection Number: 21-2-3342

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Liberty Hyde Bailey papers, 1854-2004 1870-1958
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
21-2-3342
Abstract:
Material relating to the life and work of Liberty Hyde Bailey.
Creator:
Bailey, L. H. (Liberty Hyde), 1858-1954.
Lawrence, George H. M. (George Hill Mathewson), 1910-1978.
Cornell University. Junior Naturalist Club
Quanitities:
43.4 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE

CHRONOLOGY
March 15, 1858 Liberty Hyde Bailey, Jr. born near South Haven, Michigan
1862 Sarah Harrison Bailey, Liberty Hyde Bailey, Jr.'s mother, dies
1863 Liberty Hyde Bailey, Sr. marries Maria Bridges
1873 Liberty Hyde Bailey, Jr. delivers first public speech, "Birds," to the South Haven Pomological Society and is elected Ornithologist of the organization
1877-1882 Attends Michigan Agricultural College in Lansing, Michigan; studies Botany under the instruction of Dr. William Beal; meets Annette Smith
1882 Graduates from Michigan Agricultural College with a Bachelor of Science degree
1882 Works in Springfield, Illinois as a reporter for the Morning Monitor
Feb. 1883 Moves to Cambridge, Massachusetts to work as an assistant to Harvard botanist Asa Gray
June 1883 Marries Annette Smith
1885 Accepts professorship at Michigan Agricultural College; publishes first book "Talks Afield: About Plants and the Science of Plants"
1886 Receives Master of Science degree from Michigan Agricultural College
June 29, 1887 Daughter Sara May Bailey born
1887 Invited to give a series of lectures at Cornell
1889 Begins work as a Professor of Practical and Experimental Horticulture at Cornell
Nov. 17, 1889 Daughter Ethel Zoe Bailey born
1899 Appoints Anna Botsford Comstock as first female professor at Cornell
1904 Becomes Dean of New York State College of Agriculture at Cornell
1908 Leads a Commission on Country Life at the request of President Theodore Roosevelt
1911 Appoints Martha Van Rensselaer and Flora Rose as Professors in Home Economics
1913 Retires from position of Dean; begins a Herbarium at home on Sage Place
1917-1949 Travels around the world on plant collecting expeditions
1926 Elected President of the Botanical Society of America
1935 Gives Hortorium to Cornell
1935 Daughter Sara dies
1938 Wife Annette dies
1944 Bailey's idea for a campus arboretum, botanical garden, and research field is realized with the opening of the Cornell Plantations
Mar. 15, 1948 Bailey misses 90th birthday party in Ithaca because he is on plant collecting trip in West Indies
Dec. 25, 1954 Bailey dies at his home in Ithaca
1958 United States Post Office issues a commemoration stamp honoring gardening and horticulture in America
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Personal, family, and professional papers relating to the life and work of Liberty Hyde Bailey and including correspondence, manuscripts, scrapbooks, journals, photographs, publications, genealogical and biographical information. Also records of the Bailey Hortorium. Correspondence and records of the Commission on Country Life. Also includes glass negatives of Bailiwick orchards and house construction; correspondence and agreements with Macmillan Company; photographs; Bailey's correspondence with his brother Marcus and daughter Ethel; and deeds, abstracts and title searches for the Bailey house on Sage Place; war ration books; agreement of gift of the Bailey Hortorium. Includes Bailey correspondence with Macmillan Company. Also, tape recordings (12 cassettes) and transcripts of an interview with Bailey by George H. M. Lawrence, 1951-1952, audio reels with interview by Edith Fox and Jackson Towne, 11/06/1951 and his typewriter and camera.
Includes a 1901 Junior Naturalist Club certificate signed by Bailey.
CD (MS Power Point) with posters prepared at Michigan State University using Cornell photographs, celebrating the 150th anniversary of the birth of Liberty Hyde Bailey, 2008. The posters were exhibited at the 2008 annual meetings of the American Society for Horticultural Science (July) and at the Great Lakes Expo (Michigan Horticultural Society and Michigan Vegetable Growers (December).
Printing block for the Loganberry illustration from "Gentes Herbarum," with page from the journal showing the illustration and a modern print; other various printing blocks, as well.
RELATED MATERIAL

See also Liberty Hyde Bailey, New York State College of Agriculture and Agricultural Experiment Station Records, #21-2-84, Liberty Hyde Bailey Forestry Papers, #21-2-576 and College of Agriculture "Dean's Hat" items, #21-2-3144.
Liberty Hyde Bailey: A Man for All Seasons exhibition
Liberty Hyde Bailey Hortorium
SEPARATED MATERIAL

Several items relating to Bailey including the Bailey plow, portrait painted by Henry S. Hubbell for Bailey Hall, gold medals, canes, are located in the College of Agriculture Dean's Office, Chair of Plant Science's Office, and Mann Library. The Bailey Hortorium also houses Bailey books, publications, photographs, and artwork.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Liberty Hyde Bailey Papers, #21-2-3342. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

Bailey, Marcus.
Liberty Hyde Bailey Hortorium
New York State College of Agriculture
Cornell University
United States Country Life Commission
Macmillan Company
Subjects:
Botany -- Study and teaching.
Education, Rural.
Form and Genre Terms:
Printing blocks.

Scope and Contents
Arranged in semi-chronological order

Author: L. H. Bailey; 2 copies; the essay was read before the society on September 4, 1873

three envelopes with Bailey's printed photo

Series X. Cyanotype albums and negatives
 1888 - 1910
Cyanotype albums
Processing Information
Album pages no longer remain bound with covers. Some cyanotypes removed from album pages are described separately. Copies of the information on those removed pages are included in the same locations within the albums.
3 x 4 prints
3 x 4 in
Box 35 Folder 1
Professors, students, fruits, vegetables
 1890-1897
Scope and Contents
Prints numbered 3.1-3.232. Include squash, apple, grapes, Will Wescott, Prof. Roberts, Prof. HHWing, Prof. HS Williams, Prof. James Law, Prof. JH Comstock, Wm Caldwell, Miss Smith, Mrs. Bailey, strawberry leaves, eggplants, tomatos, plums, Electric light house (green house), flowers, apples, Prof. Prentis, LH Bailey, leaves, beans, blackberries
4 x 5 prints
4 x 5 in
Box 35 Folder 2
Plants, fruit trees, flowers, travels
 1889-1891
Scope and Contents
Prints numbered 1-88. Include plums, plum trees, old homestead South Haven Mich, lillies, radishes, dewberries, peach trees in Michigan, begonias, electric light experiment, horseradish, flowers
Box 35 Folder 3
Vegetables, fruits, trees, orchards
 1889-1891
Scope and Contents
Prints numbered 89-184. Include parsley, cauliflower, strawberry plants, apples, pears, plums, cherries, chestnuts, mushroom spawn, potato plants, tomato plants, Maxwells plum and apple orchards, Willards vineyards, gooseberries, weeping beech, weeping birch, weeping basswood, weeping spruce, poplars, weeping hemlock, peach trees.
Box 35 Folder 4
Local landscapes, travels, transplanting, cutting and spraying experiments
 1889-1890
Scope and Contents
Print numbers 185-280. Includes wheelbarrow spraying tank, tree bark, spinach kale and cabbage fields in Norfolk, Ithaca Light House, Cayuga Lake, forcing house, LHB backyard, Downing Place, tomato and cucumber houses near Boston, barrens on Long Island, transplanting radishes, cutting investigation (microscopic images), root grafts, endive, grapes, grape pruning.
Box 35 Folder 5
Fruits and vegetables
 1889-1890
Scope and Contents
Prints numbered 281-376. Includes raspberries, bean plants, cabbage, pepper plants, egg plants, egg plant plants, tomatos, tomato plants, potatos, sprouting potatos.
Box 35 Folder 6
Fruits, vegetables, travels
 1889-1891
Scope and Contents
Prints numbered 377-472. Includes potatos, sprouting potatoes, cucumbers, eggplants, Peter Toner, tomatoes, apples, plums, potatos, grapes, Michigan creek and sand dunes.
Box 35 Folder 7
Travels, local views, fruits, vegetables
 1891-1892
Scope and Contents
Print numbers 473-568. Includes Michigan views, tomatoes, grapes, pepinos, eggplants, cabbage, pears, squash, turnips, plums, greenhouse, yard at Watkins NY, Botanic gardens in Cambridge MA, Cemetary at Cayuga, Cranberry Bog near Lakewood NY.
Box 35 Folder 8
Local farms and views, vegetables, fruits, flowers
 1891-1892
Scope and Contents
Prints numbered 569-665. Includes propagating frame, greenhouse, Seneca Lake at Watkins, apple orchards and grape pruning at Watkins, gladiola bulbs, carnations, roses, plums, tomatos, beans, strawberries, buffalo-berry; watermelons, greenhouses near Boston, plums, apricots, mulberries, plums, tomatos.
Box 35 Folder 9
Gourds
 1889-1891
Scope and Contents
Prints numbered 666-761

Box 35 Folder 13
Grapes and travels
 1892
Scope and Contents

Box 35 Folder 14
Fruits, vegetables, flowers, Cornell campus
 1892-1893
Scope and Contents
Prints numbered 1246-1341. Includes turnips, squash, pears, plums, cherries, apples, heat experiment, carnations, Virginia Creeper pillar outside MCGraw Hall 1893, yams, , cucumbers, corn, begonias, potatos, squash.
Box 35 Folder 15
Fruits, vegetables, flowers
 1889-1894
Scope and Contents
Prints numbered 1342-1441. Includes squash, corn, apples, strawberries, cherries, ferns, geraniums, horsechestnut blossoms, fields and orchards in Michigan, beans, radishes, cabbage.
Box 35 Folder 16
Fruits, flowers, experiments, travel
 1893-1894
Scope and Contents
Prints numbered 1442-1537. Includes orchids, begonias, carnations, electric light experiment, blackberries, grapes, peaches, strawberries, plums, pears, water ways at Lincoln Park in Chicago.
Box 35 Folder 17
Cornell campus and travels
 1893-1894
Scope and Contents
Prints numbered 1538-1633. Includes Lincoln Park Chicago, plantings/landscape at CU including Sage Terrace, Sage College Lown, Barnes Hall, Uris Library, Central Ave; pears, melons, squash; acgricultural exhibitions and buildings at World's Fair in Italy.

Box 35 Folder 22

Prints numbered 2018-2114. Includes loading strawberries for transport in Oswego, squashes, plums, blackberries, celery, brussel sprouts, sweet pea, dahlias.
Box 35 Folder 23
Strawberry transport, flowers, travels
 1894-1896
Scope and Contents
Prints numbered 2115-2218. Includes begonias in lab, strawberry shipping and marketing in Oswego, chrysanthemums, Ixia, orange groves and everglades in FL, squash flowers.
